A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

Notice Of Motion Of For Order Compelling Further Responses To Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DON JOSE BARRELIER'S MOTION OF FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND FOR SANCTIONS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant and cross-complainant Don Jose Barrelier's motion to compel further responses to form and special interrogatories from plaintiff and cross-defendant Jackie Forge, and for sanctions, is granted in part.

The court concludes that Barrelier's meet and confer efforts were sufficient on this motion. The court encourages the parties to make more extensive informal resolution efforts on future motions.

Form Rog Nos. 4.1, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.5, 12.6, 12.7, 13.1, 13.2, 14.1, 14.2: The complaint in this case relates to an alleged breach of a settlement agreement, and a demand for specific performance of the settlement agreement related to stairway construction. The cross-complaint relates to a series of alleged trespasses. The court concludes that the use of the term "incident" in these form interrogatories to encompass all of the many acts and events leading to this case is compound and vague. No further response is ordered.

Form Rog Nos. 50.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5, 50.6: Forge's objections lack merit and her responses are conclusory. She is ordered to amend to respond in full.

Special Rog Nos. 1-8, 12-19, 21-22: Forge's objections lack merit. "An interrogatory may relate to whether another party is making a certain contention, or to the facts, witnesses, and writings on which a contention is based. An interrogatory is not objectionable because an answer to it involves an opinion or contention that relates to fact or the application of law to fact, . . . ." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2023.010, subd. (b).) Interrogatories are a proper tool for a propounding party to learn what legal theories or contentions his opponent is advancing, what injunctive relief his opponent is seeking, and what facts his opponent believes support his contentions or claims for relief. Forge's cursory and boilerplate responses must be amended to answer each interrogatory in full.

Special Rog Nos. 9-11: These interrogatories are vague and overbroad. No further response is ordered.

Special Rog Nos. 20: This interrogatory seeks work product. No further response is ordered.

Further verified responses ordered herein shall be made within three weeks.

Both parties seek sanctions. Although Barrelier has not prevailed on all of the interrogatories where he sought relief, the court concludes that Forge's discovery position in failing to provide substantive information in response to proper interrogatories lacks substantial justification. Forge shall pay reasonable sanctions of $3,000 to Barrelier within 30 days.

Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
